key_insight: A deformable Cosserat framework captures strain localization in softening plasticity while keeping standard Cauchy elastoplastic constitutive models unchanged.
engineering_relevance: This lets engineers reuse existing plasticity algorithms as black-box models in finite element codes, adding only linear micro-continuum terms to improve localization prediction.
method: Embed a classical elastoplastic macro-continuum inside a deformable Cosserat micro-continuum.
Keep dissipation in the macro-scale model and use the micro-scale only for linear elastic director-field effects.
Let the internal length scale emerge from the micro-continuum to regulate localization pattern selection and interaction.
evidence_signal: Benchmark shallow-foundation simulations with Tresca and Matsuoka-Nakai plasticity showed mesh-convergent load-displacement curves, dissipated energy, and shear-band patterns, including unstable post-peak cases.
watch_item: The method still requires careful finite element implementation of the added Cosserat residual and tangent terms, especially for problems with interacting localization modes.
paper_title: Strain localization in softening plasticity without modifying standard constitutive models: a deformable Cosserat approach
